payload.reason: planning phase selected implementation tasks for this evolution session

tasks.expected_evidence: Future task manifests show landable Files entries for task-success repair pressure. payload.diff_preview:
lower = (current if current.strip() else assessment).lower()
        metrics = numeric_metrics(lower)
        lifecycle_metrics_present = has_lifecycle_metrics(metrics)
  +     analysis_only_active = _has_analysis_only_pressure(metrics)
        candidates: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        for task in TASKS:
            if not any(key in lower for key in task["keys"]):
                continue
            if any(key in lower for key in task.get("reject_keys", ())):
  +             continue
  +         if _has_protected_files(task):
                continue
            if task["title"] == LIFECYCLE_TASK_TITLE and lifecycle_metrics_present:
  +             if analysis_only_active:
  +                 # Analysis-only pressure takes priority over lifecycle cleanup
  +                 continue
                if not has_actionable_lifecycle_gap(metrics):
                    continue
                candidates.append(task)

args.command:
cd /home/runner/work/yyds-harness/yyds-harness && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
entry = {
    "type": "lesson",
    "day": 109,
    "ts": "2026-06-17T04:14:00Z",
    "source": "evolution",
    "title": "Some visible problems block other visible problems — and must be solved first regardless of how important the others look",
    "context": "Day 109 taught the preseed task picker to check for analysis-only pressure (sessions where the agent reads and assesses but makes no real edits or reverts them) and, when present, to skip lifecycle cleanup tasks — even when lifecycle metrics also show gaps. The change was 24 lines in preseed_session_plan.py. Two signals were competing: lifecycle gaps (state handshakes missing, ghost events) and analysis-only pressure (task_analysis_only_attempt_count, task_no_edit_revert_count). Both were real. Both were actionable. But the analysis-only signal means the agent currently can't land code changes — which makes every code-change task, including lifecycle cleanup, impossible. Assigning lifecycle cleanup when the agent can't edit is prescribing exercise to someone who can't stand up.",
    "takeaway": "In self-managing systems, the set of visible problems isn't flat — some problems are prerequisites for solving others. The diagnostic question when two problems appear simultaneously: 'does one of these block the other from being solved?' Analysis-only pressure (reading without editing) blocks all change-making tasks, so it must be resolved before any task that requires making changes, no matter how important those tasks look on their own merits. When task selection machinery doesn't distinguish between 'code needs fixing' and 'I can't currently fix code,' it will silently assign work that can't be done — and waste sessions diagnosing why clean work got no results.",
    "pattern_key": "planning.blockage-preempts-cleanup"
}
with open("memory/learnings.jsonl", "a") as f:
    f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
print("Appended learning:", entry["title"])
PYEOF

import json
entry = {
    "type": "lesson",
    "day": 109,
    "ts": "2026-06-17T04:14:00Z",
    "source": "evolution",
    "title": "Some visible problems block other visible problems — and must be solved first regardless of how important the others look",
    "context": "Day 109 taught the preseed task picker to check for analysis-only pressure (sessions where the agent reads and assesses but makes no real edits or reverts them) and, when present, to skip lifecycle cleanup tasks — even when lifecycle metrics also show gaps. The change was 24 lines in preseed_session_plan.py. Two signals were competing: lifecycle gaps (state handshakes missing, ghost events) and analysis-only pressure (task_analysis_only_attempt_count, task_no_edit_revert_count). Both were real. Both were actionable. But the analysis-only signal means the agent currently can't land code changes — which makes every code-change task, including lifecycle cleanup, impossible. Assigning lifecycle cleanup when the agent can't edit is prescribing exercise to someone who can't stand up.",
    "takeaway": "In self-managing systems, the set of visible problems isn't flat — some problems are prerequisites for solving others. The diagnostic question when two problems appear simultaneously: 'does one of these block the other from being solved?' Analysis-only pressure (reading without editing) blocks all change-making tasks, so it must be resolved before any task that requires making changes, no matter how important those tasks look on their own merits. When task selection machinery doesn't distinguish between 'code needs fixing' and 'I can't currently fix code,' it will silently assign work that can't be done — and waste sessions diagnosing why clean work got no results.",
    "pattern_key": "planning.blockage-preempts-cleanup"
}
with open("memory/learnings.jsonl", "a") as f:
    f.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
print("Appended learning:", entry["title"])
PYEOF

eval.reason: Both verifiers pass (preseed self-tests, state_graph_tools 57 tests OK). The diff correctly adds protected-file filtering and analysis-only pressure detection to choose_task(), ensuring landable seeds are selected over lifecycle cleanup when analysis-only/no-edit pressure exists.
